last_commit: c58a38a64e3e - bpf: Fix RT task tracking - retain tasks regardless of __state

bpf_rt_task_fix.description: Fixed critical bug in BPF queue_track backend where RT tasks were being incorrectly removed from tracking queue
bpf_rt_task_fix.problem: RT tasks (SCHED_FIFO, SCHED_RR, SCHED_DEADLINE) were filtered out based on __state != TASK_RUNNING, causing starvation_gen test workloads to not be detected
bpf_rt_task_fix.root_cause:
update_or_add_task() had TWO locations filtering by task_running():
  1. Line 255 (update path): Removed existing tasks if not in TASK_RUNNING state
  2. Line 279 (add path): Rejected new tasks if not in TASK_RUNNING state
RT tasks on runqueue waiting for CPU may not have __state == TASK_RUNNING during tracepoint execution
bpf_rt_task_fix.solution: Modified both locations to use `task_running(p) || task_is_rt(p)` to keep RT tasks in queue regardless of __state
bpf_rt_task_fix.impact: queue_track backend now successfully detects SCHED_FIFO starvation. Manual testing confirms 'stalld: found task: starvation_gen:PID starving' messages now appear

test_status.detection_working: YES - starvation_gen tasks are now detected by queue_track backend
test_status.tests_passing: PARTIAL - detection works but some tests fail due to blockees not completing work (separate issue)
verified_tests: Manual verification with verbose stalld shows RT task detection working
test_fifo_boosting.sh shows improved detection (still fails on blockee completion)

blockees_not_completing.description: starvation_gen blockees complete 0/N iterations even when starvation is detected and boosting occurs
possible_causes: Boost duration too short relative to work target
Separate boosting/scheduling issue unrelated to BPF detection
blockees_not_completing.status: NOT YET INVESTIGATED - detection is fixed, this is a different problem

next_steps: Investigate why blockees don't complete work despite detection and boosting
Consider adjusting test work targets or boost durations
Run full test suite to verify no regressions from BPF changes
May need to examine boost_with_deadline() and boost_with_fifo() logic

technical_notes.bpf_task_states: RT tasks on runqueue may have transient __state values during tracepoint execution. Cannot rely solely on TASK_RUNNING (0) for RT task tracking.
technical_notes.task_is_rt: Checks p->prio >= 0 && p->prio <= 99 (kernel RT priority range). SCHED_FIFO priority 1 maps to kernel prio 98.
tracepoints_involved: sched_wakeup_new - fires when new task created/woken
sched_wakeup - fires when task woken from sleep

starvation_gen.description: Configurable starvation generator for controlled testing
starvation_gen.blocker_priority: 80 (kernel prio 19) - high priority SCHED_FIFO task that monopolizes CPU
starvation_gen.blockee_priority: 1 (kernel prio 98) - low priority SCHED_FIFO tasks that starve
starvation_gen.work_target: 100000000 iterations (default) - may be too high for current boost durations
starvation_gen.status: Working correctly - creates starvation as designed
